agora_post.title: [Hepatobiliary and Pancreatic Surgery] 任务 #8473
agora_post.description: The patient was diagnosed with chronic Hepatitis B during a physical examination 20 years ago but did not receive standardized treatment. About one year ago, without obvious precipitating factors, the patient experienced upper abdominal pain. The patient visited a local hospital where 'primary liver cancer' was suspected, and subsequently visited an oncology hospital where the diagnosis of 'primary liver cancer (consistent with hepatocellular carcinoma)' was confirmed. The patient received oral 'Lenvatinib' for 10 months and underwent Transarterial Chemoembolization (TACE) 8 times. Currently, the patient is on long-term oral targeted therapy with 'Regorafenib.' An upper abdominal MRI (plain scan plus contrast-enhanced) performed at our outpatient department indicated: 1. Post-treatment changes in a large mixed-signal multinodular lesion in the right hepatic lobe; most of the lesion shows no obvious enhancement. However, clumped/patchy enhancement is noted around a lesion at the lateral aspect of the hepatic dome, suggesting residual viable tumor in that area. 2. A quasi-round soft-tissue lesion with mixed signal intensity is seen filling the gallbladder fossa, appearing to be connected to the cystic duct; cholelithiasis cannot be excluded; please correlate with clinical history and relevant examinations. 3. Left renal cyst. Alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) measurement [2022-6-17 11:00:33]: AFP 1,920.000 ng/mL. Please provide a suitable treatment plan for this patient along with the rationale.

plan_agent.input_text:
[Hepatobiliary and Pancreatic Surgery] 任务 #8473
The patient was diagnosed with chronic Hepatitis B during a physical examination 20 years ago but did not receive standardized treatment. About one year ago, without obvious precipitating factors, the patient experienced upper abdominal pain. The patient visited a local hospital where 'primary liver cancer' was suspected, and subsequently visited an oncology hospital where the diagnosis of 'primary liver cancer (consistent with hepatocellular carcinoma)' was confirmed. The patient received oral

sub_tasks.question: Evaluate the current status of the patient's hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) based on the MRI findings and AFP level. Specifically, describe the extent of residual viable tumor, the significance of the gallbladder fossa lesion, and the overall tumor burden.

Review the patient's prior treatments (Lenvatinib, 8 TACE sessions, current Regorafenib) and assess their efficacy and the patient's tolerance. Determine if there is evidence of disease progression or treatment resistance.

Based on the tumor status and prior treatment history, propose a suitable treatment plan. Consider options such as local ablation (e.g., RFA, MWA), radiation therapy (SBRT), further TACE, systemic therapy modification (e.g., switching to atezolizumab/bevacizumab or other targeted/immunotherapy combinations), or surgical resection if feasible. Provide rationale for the recommended approach.

final_synthesis.instruction: Integrate the findings from sub-tasks A, B, and C to produce a comprehensive treatment plan. The plan should include specific recommendations (e.g., local ablation for residual tumor, systemic therapy adjustment), the rationale based on tumor burden and prior treatment response, and any additional considerations such as gallbladder fossa lesion management or renal cyst follow-up.

rounds.answer_preview:
好的，作为肝胆胰外科专家，我将整合您提供的子任务A（病情评估）、B（局部治疗建议）和C（系统治疗调整）的分析结果，形成一份综合治疗计划，并给出每项选择的循证医学依据。

### 综合治疗计划

**患者概况：** 慢性乙肝背景的肝癌患者，既往接受仑伐替尼+TACE一线治疗，当前影像学显示肝顶外侧缘簇状/片状残留活性病灶，并伴有胆囊窝软组织病变（需鉴别肿瘤侵犯或良性病变）。患者正在使用瑞戈非尼作为二
